data.name: Claude Code chunk-done push notifications
data.description: Get a phone push when a Claude Code session finishes a chunk of work, so I can step away from the desk and know exactly when (and which terminal) to come back to.
data.notes:
• A Stop hook already pings my iPhone via ntfy.sh when a session ends a chunk — always on, mute from the phone
• THE PROBLEM TO SOLVE: every open session pings me, and they all look the same — I got 'come back' alerts for the AI hub while I was actually working in zvs-edge, with no way to tell sessions apart
• Want the alert to show the terminal/chat name (the title the terminal auto-makes from my first message), not just the project folder
• Catch: the hook can't read the terminal tab title — it only gets the folder + session id; need another way to label each session (likely capture its first prompt)
• Ideal end state: only the session(s) I care about ping me, each clearly labeled, one mute switch on the phone

data.name: Mithoo — Flappy Bird game
data.description: A Flappy Bird–style game made to honor Mithoo, my pet bird.
data.notes:
• Tap to flap Mithoo through gaps between obstacles
• Built as a little tribute to my bird — use his real colors/look for the character
• Simple one-tap gameplay; score = obstacles passed, best score saved
• Fun touches: a chirp sound on each flap, themed backgrounds
• Can be a lightweight web/phone game — no app store needed

data.name: Subway Surfers–style runner (for my girlfriend)
data.description: An endless runner game my girlfriend can play offline on her way to work.
data.notes:
• Swipe to dodge obstacles and grab coins; it speeds up as you go
• Must work fully offline (no signal on the commute)
• Personalize it for her — character, theme, maybe some inside jokes
• Quick pick-up-and-play sessions; high scores saved on the phone
• Simple touch controls, designed for holding the phone upright

data.name: AI beginner journey newsletter
data.description: A newsletter sharing my beginner AI journey with Claude — and hopefully making some money from it.
data.notes:
• Write articles about learning AI as a beginner, specifically using Claude
• Use Claude to help brainstorm, draft, and edit each post
• Pick a platform with built-in subscriptions (like Substack or beehiiv)
• Make money later through paid subscribers or sponsors once there's an audience
• Share real projects (like this hub) as honest behind-the-scenes case studies

data.name: Algo trading with Claude
data.description: Work with Claude to build some kind of model to help with the stock market / trading.
data.notes:
• Explore 'algo trading' — automated, rule-based buy/sell strategies
• Use Claude and Claude Code to research, prototype, and test ideas
• Start simple and paper-trade (fake money) first to see if it actually works
• Learn the basics: where to get data, indicators, and managing risk
• Keep it as a learning experiment early on — real trading carries real risk

data.name: ZV's Edge
data.description: An AI sports betting app for my iPhone that helps spot good bets to make.
data.nextStep: Neil: close/reopen the PWA to pull the new build (service-worker cache) — then confirm the Parlay + Next Best sections show even when empty. Still open: knockout market choice (Match Result vs To Qualify) + reconnect Railway/Vercel GitHub auto-deploy webhooks.
data.lastDid: Fixed the 'parlay/next-best removed' complaint: the BACKEND was correct (only 3 games left today; just 2 picks clear the 5% edge bar, dark-horse needs >=10% edge, and IC×Sweden joint hit-chance ~4% is below the 10% parlay floor — so the model rightly forced nothing). The bug was the FRONTEND hiding empty sections entirely. Home now always renders Next Best + Parlay with clear empty states ('model won't force one') instead of vanishing. Deployed to Vercel.
data.notes: Live and feature-rich (picks, locked POTD + switch flow, live tracker, push). Now backed up to GitHub.

log.text: Parlay/next-best 'disappeared' was a frontend empty-state bug, not data loss. Backend correctly declined to force a parlay (joint prob ~4% < 10% floor) or dark horse (edge < 10%). Home now always shows those sections with an explanation when empty. Deployed.

Model card +P&L, settlement void/unmatched safety nets (audit #1+#2), and POTD validity confirmed. Clarified Neil's odds confusion: app's +260 = Match Result (90-min win); FanDuel's +144 he saw = To Qualify (incl ET/pens) — different markets. Odds are fetched on-demand (fresh), board hardens ~30min pre-kickoff. Open product decision: knockout market choice (Match Result vs To Qualify).

Fixed penalty-shootout score ingestion (fullTime included pen kicks -> 1-1 game read as 4-5). Verified live: feed now 1-1, Germany record 2-1-1. Audited the rest of the settlement path; flagged abandoned-match void gap + team-name join failures as the next silent-failure risks.

Shipped the whole-board lock + tracker fixes to prod. Root-caused the recurring data wipe: no Railway volume — attached one at /data so the DB persists. Caught that neither Railway nor Vercel auto-deployed the last push; deployed both by CLI. Model record seeded 1-0 from the Germany/Paraguay BTTS hit (logged placed=0, settled off the real results feed).

Backend session: folded auto-settlement into run_alert_check so finished bets grade automatically (it was stuck behind ENABLE_SCHEDULER, off in prod to save odds credits). Settlement runs first + unconditionally on the free results feed so the Tracker P&L stays current; added a 'bet settled' push for placed bets reusing tracker.bet_profit_units; and made settle_pending short-circuit when nothing's pending. Compile + smoke tested, pushed to main. Open question to confirm: ENABLE_ALERTS must be on in Railway for the loop to run. Next up: Neil's Home page changes.

Rewrote the stale 'scaffold only' README to reflect the live feature-complete app (model, picks, push, tracker, deploy) and pushed it. Scoped the next phase with Neil: work ONLY on Home + Tracker, ignore Lines/Bracket/Analysis. Backend audit of the live Railway service found three real gaps that matter for those two pages: auto-settlement isn't running (settle job gated behind disabled scheduler), scheduled odds ticks are off (odds refresh on app-open only), and CLV is a hard stub (NotImplementedError, blocked on OddsPapi API docs). Agreed priority: fix settlement first (fold into the alert loop that's already on), then a Home/Tracker design pass using Neil's feedback.

Created the private GitHub repo zvcodez/zvs-edge and pushed main. The remote was already wired over SSH, just needed the empty repo on GitHub's side — code is now versioned and backed up off the machine.

Major post-launch session. Renamed to a clean domain (zvs-edge.vercel.app). Fixed the things Neil flagged: stale odds now refresh on load, picks scoped to TODAY's slate (no more tomorrow's game as POTD), FanDuel price leads with a DraftKings 'better line' flag, concrete bet-specific 'why' + parlay reasoning, bankroll asks on first launch ($400), freshness stamp, bracket explainer. Built Web Push end-to-end (VAPID + 5 toggles + bet-aware alerts). Locked the Play of the Day so it never auto-changes; added an in-app Update section that suggests a stronger play (did-you-place → switch, or cash-out-vs-ride) with why-better bullets and a switch history; and a live in-play tracker showing how close the bet is to hitting. Backend on Railway, frontend on Vercel — all deployed and verified.

Launched the app live on the internet — backend on Railway, frontend on Vercel, wired together. Hit and fixed a few hosting snags (Python version, packaging). The live app now pulls real World Cup picks from the model (today’s Play of the Day: Germany vs Paraguay, Both Teams To Score). Next: add to iPhone home screen + notifications.

Got everything ready to put the app online — set up the hosting files and wrote a step-by-step guide for launching it. Still just the basic skeleton at this point: no betting features built yet, and no GitHub repo connected.
Started the project — an AI sports betting app for my iPhone to help spot good bets. Scaffolded the app structure to build on.

data.name: Neil AI Hub
data.description: My personal dashboard that keeps track of all my AI projects in one place.
data.nextStep: Make the project private so my personal notes aren't public.
data.notes: One place to keep an eye on all my AI projects.

log.text: Big cleanup session. Removed the standalone Ideas tab (ideas now live in Projects), changed the logo from NAH to NP, and rewrote all the project text in plain, everyday language. Added a stage filter at the top, a 'move to stage' option in each card's menu, bulleted notes, and a new 'open details' view with a journal. Also trimmed the stages down to just Idea / Building / Live, and added four new ideas (Mithoo game, runner game for my girlfriend, AI newsletter, algo trading).
Cross-device sync working — each device pulls and pushes its data through GitHub, so the hub stays in sync between my phone and Mac.
Built and launched the hub — a personal dashboard to keep an eye on all my AI projects in one place. Live on GitHub Pages.

data.name: Neil Portfolio
data.description: My personal portfolio website — about me, experience, skills, education, plus a little Q&A chat bot. It's live online.
data.lastDid: Checked that the site is live and working, and tidied up its info here.
data.nextStep: Pick a few finishing touches: a smarter chat bot, a downloadable resume, or fixing a location link that doesn't go anywhere.

log.text: Confirmed the site is live and working on GitHub Pages, and tidied up its info here in the hub. Ideas for finishing touches: a smarter chat bot, a downloadable resume, or fixing a location link that goes nowhere.
Built my personal portfolio site — about me, experience, skills, education, and a little Q&A chat bot. Launched it on GitHub Pages.
